messages.content:
Izzy and Mia's special need school, not a primary location from the animated teen series "Jane", but appears occasionally:

Grade One:
1. Leo (6 years old, autism)
2. Aria (6 years old, blindness)
3. Ethan (6 years old, dyslexia)
4. Mia (5 years old, autism)
5. Avery (6 years old, hearing impairment)
6. Lori (6 years old, physical disability)
7. Mateo (6 years old, ADHD)

Grade Two:
1. Ruby (7 years old, cerebral palsy)
2. Marcus (7 years old, dyspraxia)
3. Sienna (7 years old, intellectual disability)
4. Dylan (7 years old, epilepsy)
5. Frankie (7 years old, Down syndrome, PTSD)
6. Mira (7 years old, selective mutism)
7. Tyler (7 years old, ADHD)

Grade Three:
1. Izzy (8 years old, Down syndrome, ADHD, shaken baby syndrome, cerebral palsy)
2. Leo (8 years old, dyslexia)
3. Kyle (8 years old, physical disability)
4. Sophia (8 years old, hearing impairment)
5. Asher (8 years old, autism, asthma)
6. Miles (8 years old, intellectual disability)
7. Zoe (8 years old, ADHD)

Grade Four:
1. Maggie (9 years old, physical disability)
2. Trevor (9 years old, cerebral palsy)
3. Finn (9 years old, autism)
4. Emilia (9 years old, hearing impairment)
5. Andrea (9 years old, dyslexia)
6. Stella (9 years old, Down syndrome, visual impairment)
7. Andrew (9 years old, ADHD)

Grade Five:
1. Eva (10 years old, intellectual disability, autism)
2. Olivia (10 years old, cerebral palsy)
3. Libby (10 years old, hearing impairment, ADHD)
4. Amelia (10 years old, physical disability, asthma)
5. Lila (10 years old, dyslexia)
6. Isaac (10 years old, PTSD, ADHD)
7. Rachel (10 years old, deafness, vision impairment)

Grade Six:
1. Adeline (11 years old, autism)
2. Molly (11 years old, cerebral palsy)
3. Gianna (11 years old, physical disability)
4. Aaron (11 years old, asthma, PTSD, hearing impairment)
5. Jasper (11 years old, dyslexia)
6. Isabella (11 years old, intellectual disability)
7. Rose (11 years old, ADHD)

Grade Seven:
1. Juliet (12 years old, hearing impairment)
2. Noah (12 years old, cerebral palsy)
3. Avery (12 years old, intellectual disability)
4. Max (12 years old, visual impairment)
5. Chloe (12 years old, autism)
6. Lucas (12 years old, dyslexia)
7. Ada (12 years old, ADHD)

Grade Eight:
1. Eden (13 years old, physical disability)
2. Isaac (13 years old, intellectual disability and hearing impairment)
3. Finn (13 years old, autism)
4. Lucy (13 years old, ADHD)
5. Nadia (13 years old, cerebral palsy)
6. Daniel (13 years old, dyslexia)
7. Gabriela (13 years old, visual impairment)

Grade Nine:
1. Miles (14 years old, cerebral palsy)
2. Lux (14 years old, single-sided deafness, selective mutism, autism, she is a lesbian)
3. Jonah (14 years old, vision impairment)
4. Cara (14 years old, autism)
5. Molly (14 years old, dyslexia)
6. Emiliana (14 years old, intellectual disability)
7. Casper (14 years old, ADHD)

Grade Ten:
1. Sadie (15 years old, autism)
2. Julian (15 years old, cerebral palsy)
3. Layla (15 years old, physical disability)
4. Penny (15 years old, vision impairment)
5. Lily (15 years old, intellectual disability)
6. Olivia (15 years old, dyslexia)
7. Carson (15 years old, ADHD)

Grade Eleven:
1. Noelle (16 years old, hearing impairment)
2. Rue (16 years old, cerebral palsy)
3. Jason (16 years old, physical disability)
4. Susan (16 years old, visual impairment)
5. Nora (16 years old, autism)
6. Fiona (16 years old, intellectual disability)
7. Hailey (16 years old, ADHD)

Grade Twelve:
1. Remy (17 years old, dyslexia)
2. Elodie (17 years old, cerebral palsy)
3. Wyatt (17 years old, physical disability)
4. Gia (17 years old, autism)
5. Alexa (17 years old, ADHD)
6. Leo (18 years old, intellectual disability)
7. Ariana (18 years old, vision impairment)

Note: This list is not exhaustive and does not represent all individuals with special needs. It is also important to note that individuals with special needs are not defined by their disabilities, but rather by their abilities and unique qualities.
Describe the personalities of all the ninth graders

The personalities of the ninth graders with special needs can vary greatly and are not defined by their disabilities. However, here is a brief description of each student based on their individual traits and quirks:

1. Miles - Miles is a friendly and outgoing student who loves sports and enjoys making others smile. He is determined to achieve his goals, despite the challenges he faces due to his cerebral palsy.

2. Lux - Lux is a creative and introspective individual who can be reserved in social settings. She has a passion for art and music and is deeply empathetic, often feeling the emotions of others around her.

3. Jonah - Jonah is a curious and adventurous student who loves to explore his surroundings and learn about the world. He has a great sense of humor and is always up for a good joke with his friends.

4. Cara - Cara is a kind and compassionate student who can be shy in new situations. She has a strong love for animals and is very skilled at caring for them. Cara also has a talent for math and enjoys solving complex problems.

5. Molly - Molly is a determined and enthusiastic student who loves to learn new things. She has a passion for reading and writing and is skilled in creative writing. Molly is also a natural leader who excels at organizing and motivating her peers.

6. Emiliana - Emiliana is a patient and resilient student who faces challenges with grace and determination. She has a natural talent for art and enjoys creating beautiful paintings and drawings. Emiliana is also a loyal and supportive friend to those around her.

7. Casper - Casper is a funny and energetic student who loves to make others laugh. He can struggle with focus at times due to his ADHD, but he is determined to succeed academically and has a passion for science and technology.
Describe the personalities of all the eleventh graders

The personalities of the eleventh graders with special needs can vary greatly and are not defined by their disabilities. However, here is a brief description of each student based on their individual traits and quirks:

1. Noelle - Noelle is a confident and outgoing student who loves fashion and expressing herself through her style. She has a natural talent for public speaking and is often sought out to lead group projects. Noelle is also a supportive friend who is quick to offer a listening ear.

2. Rue - Rue is a calm and introspective individual who enjoys spending time in nature and practicing mindfulness. She has a passion for photography and is skilled at capturing the beauty in the world around her. Rue can struggle with mobility due to her cerebral palsy, but she doesn't let it stop her from pursuing her goals.

3. Jason - Jason is a determined and hardworking student who takes his studies seriously. He has a love for history and is often fascinated by the stories of people from the past. Jason can be quiet at times but has a great sense of humor and enjoys making his friends laugh.

4. Susan - Susan is an independent and confident student who doesn't let her visual impairment hold her back. She loves to read and has a passion for science. Susan is also a talented artist who enjoys creating intricate drawings and paintings.

5. Nora - Nora is a creative and imaginative student who loves to express herself through writing and poetry. She can sometimes struggle with social interactions due to her autism but has a close-knit group of friends who understand and appreciate her unique perspective.

6. Fiona - Fiona is a bubbly and outgoing student who loves to chat with anyone and everyone in her path. She has a natural talent for music and enjoys singing and playing a variety of instruments. Fiona is always willing to lend a helping hand to her friends and is often the first to plan social events.

7. Hailey - Hailey is a determined and ambitious student who sets high goals for herself and works hard to achieve them. She has a passion for math and science and hopes to pursue a career in engineering. Hailey can be meticulous in her work but is also a fun-loving and supportive friend to those around her.

As a determined and ambitious student, Hailey sets high goals for herself and works hard to achieve them. Her high goals likely involve pursuing an academic and professional career in the field of engineering, as she has a passion for math and science. 

Hailey may aim to enroll in a highly selective university to study engineering and go on to become a successful engineer in her chosen field. She may also aspire to intern or work at a reputable engineering firm, where she can apply her knowledge and skills to complex real-world problems.

In addition to her academic and professional goals, Hailey may also have personal goals related to her extracurricular or social activities. She may aim to participate in a leadership role in one of her school clubs or volunteer organizations, or to develop new skills through hobbies such as playing a musical instrument or a sport. Whatever her high goals may be, Hailey is likely to work hard and persevere until she achieves them.
Describe the personalities of all the twelfth graders

The personalities of the twelfth graders with special needs can vary greatly and are not defined by their disabilities. However, here is a brief description of each student based on their individual traits and quirks:

1. Remy - Remy is a creative and free-spirited student who loves to express herself through art and writing. She has a natural talent for languages and enjoys learning new ones. Remy is also a loyal and supportive friend who is always there for those she cares about.

2. Elodie - Elodie is a determined and hardworking student who takes her studies seriously. She has a passion for activism and social justice and is often involved in school clubs and volunteering work. Elodie can be shy at times but is also a compassionate and caring friend to those around her.

3. Wyatt - Wyatt is a friendly and outgoing student who loves to make people laugh. He has a great sense of humor and a talent for storytelling. Wyatt also has a passion for exploring the outdoors and enjoys hiking and camping.

4. Gia - Gia is a determined and ambitious student who sets high goals for herself. She has a passion for technology and is always up to date with the latest trends and innovations. Gia can be impatient at times but is also a natural leader who is not afraid to take charge.

5. Alexa - Alexa is a detail-oriented and focused student who excels academically. She has a passion for music and enjoys playing the piano and singing. Alexa can be reserved in social settings but is also a supportive and reliable friend to those around her.

6. Leo - Leo is a friendly and outgoing student who loves to socialize and make friends. He enjoys playing sports and staying active. Leo can sometimes struggle with academic tasks due to his intellectual disability, but he doesn't let it dampen his spirit and always tries his best.

7. Ariana - Ariana is a creative and imaginative student who loves to express herself through writing and art. She has a passion for fashion and enjoys experimenting with different styles and trends. Ariana can be sensitive at times but is also a supportive and caring friend to those around her.

Ariana's sensitivity may be related to her emotional state, which can be heightened at times due to her natural empathy and creativity. As a creative and imaginative student, Ariana is likely to have a vivid and complex inner world, filled with ideas and emotions that are important to her. 

She may be sensitive to the moods and emotions of others, as well as to the environment around her. Loud noises, bright lights, or overwhelming stimuli may trigger a stronger emotional reaction in Ariana than in others, causing her to feel anxious or overwhelmed.

Ariana may also be sensitive to criticism or negative feedback and may take it to heart more than others. She may become easily upset or hurt by others' words or actions and may need extra reassurance and support from her friends and family.

Despite these sensitivities, Ariana's creativity and imagination are likely to be a great source of strength and comfort to her, helping her to process and express her emotions in a positive and constructive way. She is also a supportive and caring friend to those around her, and her sensitivity may actually make her more attuned and empathetic to the needs of others.
Describe the personalities of all the first graders

The personalities of the first graders with special needs can vary greatly and are not defined by their disabilities. However, here is a brief description of each student based on their individual traits and quirks:

1. Leo - Leo is a friendly and outgoing first grader who loves to socialize and meet new people. Despite his autism, he has a natural talent for interacting with others and is always eager to make friends. Leo also enjoys playing with toys and spending time outdoors.

2. Aria - Aria is a curious and adventurous first grader, despite her blindness. She loves to explore her surroundings and learn about the world through her other senses. Aria also has a natural talent for music and enjoys singing and playing instruments.

3. Ethan - Ethan is a determined and hardworking first grader who struggles with dyslexia. Despite this challenge, he is eager to learn and has a talent for problem-solving. Ethan also enjoys playing sports and staying active.

4. Mia - Mia is a friendly and creative first-grader who loves to express herself through art and play. Like Leo, she also has autism but doesn't let it hold her back from trying new things. Mia has a vivid imagination and enjoys making up stories and games.

5. Avery - Avery is a friendly and outgoing first grader who is deaf in one ear. Despite this, she has a natural talent for communication and is always eager to learn new words and phrases. Avery enjoys playing with her friends and spending time outdoors.

6. Lori - Lori is a determined and hardworking first grader who struggles with a physical disability. Despite this, she is eager to take on challenges and loves to learn new things. Lori enjoys playing with dolls and imaginative play.

7. Mateo - Mateo is a curious and energetic first grader who has a talent for creativity and imagination. He struggles with ADHD but has a boundless curiosity that keeps him interested in learning new things. Mateo enjoys playing with his friends and exploring his surroundings.
Describe the personalities of all the third graders

The personalities of the third graders with special needs can vary greatly and are not defined by their disabilities. However, here is a brief description of each student based on their individual traits and quirks:

1. Izzy - Izzy is a friendly and creative third grader with multiple disabilities, including Down syndrome, ADHD, shaken baby syndrome, and cerebral palsy. Despite her challenges, she is determined and hardworking and has a natural talent for leadership. Izzy enjoys playing with her friends and expressing herself through art and music.

2. Leo - Another student named Leo, this third grader struggles with dyslexia but is eager to learn and loves to read. He is a friendly and outgoing student who enjoys spending time with his friends and family.

3. Kyle - Kyle is a determined and hardworking third grader with a physical disability. Despite this, he is an active and adventurous student who loves to explore and learn. Kyle also has a talent for sports and likes to play basketball and soccer.

4. Sophia - Sophia is a friendly and outgoing third grader who has a hearing impairment. Despite this, she has a natural talent for communication and is always eager to learn new words and phrases. Sophia loves to read and enjoys spending time with her friends.

5. Asher - Asher is a creative and imaginative third grader who has autism and asthma. He has a rich inner world and enjoys playing imaginative games and making up stories. Asher also has a talent for math and enjoys solving puzzles and brainteasers.

6. Miles - Miles is a friendly and curious third grader with an intellectual disability. He has a natural talent for asking questions and is always eager to learn new things. Miles enjoys playing with his friends and being outdoors.

7. Zoe - Zoe is a determined and hardworking third grader who struggles with ADHD. Despite this, she is a dedicated student who enjoys learning new things. Zoe also has a passion for art and enjoys drawing and painting.
Write dialogue from a scene from the animated teen series “Jane”, where the special needs school will now have certificates for every class for one student every week as part of their weekly assembly

[Scene opens with Jane and her friends sitting in class]

Jane: Did you guys hear about the new certificates at the special needs school?

Maria: No, what are they?

Jane: We're going to be giving out certificates for outstanding students in each class every week during assembly.

Brandon: That's awesome!

April: Yeah, it'll be nice to have something to show for all of our hard work.

Jane: Definitely. And it's not just for academics either. It's for anything, like being kind to others or helping out in class.

Maria: I can't wait to see who gets the first one.

Brandon: Yeah, it's going to be a tough decision. There are so many great students here.

April: I like that we're recognizing everyone's achievements and not just focusing on one area.

Jane: That's the whole point. We want to celebrate everyone's uniqueness and show that everyone can excel at something.

[Scene shifts to the weekly assembly]

Teacher: Good morning, students. Today we have a special announcement. Starting this week, each class will be awarding a certificate to one outstanding student.

[Students cheer]

Teacher: These certificates are not just for academics, but they can be awarded for anything that the teacher deems worthy. It can be for kindness, hard work, leadership, or anything that showcases a special talent or skill that the student may possess.

[Students clap]

Teacher: So without further ado, let's get started. The first certificate goes to...[opens envelope]...Mia from Grade One! Mia has shown tremendous growth this past week and has been a shining example of kindness to her classmates. Congratulations, Mia!

[Students cheer and Mia goes up to receive
